MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - CROSS DEFENDANTs NOB HILL CONSTRUCTION, INC.,and AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-Defendants Nob Hill Construction, Inc. and American Contractors Indemnity Company's motion to strike Cross-complainants Harlan L. South and Kadijeh T South's cross-complaint is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. In light of the court's ruling on the companion demurrer, the court declines to adjudicate the issues raised in this motion at this time.

Cross-Defendants to submit a proposed order tracking the substance of the tentative verbatim.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - CROSS DEFENDANTs NOB HILL CONSTRUCTION, INC., and AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY, DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

The demurrer is sustained to the entire Cross Complaint based on Cross-Complainant's failure to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 2.112(4). This defect is so obvious and indisputable the court has trouble understanding how, if the parties were taking their meet and confer obligations seriously, they could not resolve the issue without court intervention. Should there be subsequent pleadings or motions requiring meet and confer, the court will closely review the meet and confer declaration to ensure compliance. The court need not-and does not-adjudicate Cross-defendants' other contentions at this time.

Cross-Complainants are granted leave to amend. In their First Amended Cross Complaint, Cross-Complainants shall explicitly indicate for each cause of action, the party or parties from whom relief is sought.

Cross-Complainants shall have 20 days from notice of this order to file a first amended cross-complaint.

Cross-Defendants to submit a proposed order tracking the substance of the tentative verbatim.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
